recent_candidates.title: Erdogan Dreams of Annexing Arab Countries, Reviving the Turkish Empire
recent_candidates.excerpt: No Images? Click here https://fdd-new.cmail19.com/t/r-e-tutdjddy-oophjwr-e/ July 14, 2026 | This Is Beirut Erdogan Dreams of Annexing Arab Countries, Reviving the Turkish Empire [https://www.fdd.org/analysis/2026/07/14/erdogan-dreams-of-annexing-arab-countries-reviving-the-turkish-empire/] The prospect of a revived Turkish empire poses a new threat to Arab sovereignty. Hussain Abdul-Hussain [https://www.fdd.org/team/hussain-abdul-hussain/] | Research Fellow Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. (Utku Ucrak/Anadolu via Getty Images) When Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan presented Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam with a copy of his grandfather Salim Salam’s memoirs, he was making more than a diplomatic gesture. By invoking the elder Salam’s service in the Ottoman parliament, Erdogan cast himself as the legitimate heir to the Ottoman sultans and the Salams as subjects of the empire. This encounter was emblematic of a larger and more troubling ambition. Erdogan is determined to revive Turkish influence over Arab lands once occupied by the Ottomans. Turkey’s aspirations extend well beyond Lebanon. On June 7, Turkish Interior Minister Mustafa Ciftci openly said he hoped to one

recent_candidates.title: Young adults are poor despite every metric which suggests otherwise
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://becomingnoble.substack.com/p/young-adults-are-poor-despite-every A recent article from The Cut received a huge amount of attention: ‘It’s Hard to See My Parents Live So Lavishly While We’re Struggling’. [ https://substack.com/redirect/1e8e411a-6ea1-49b1-a74b-a7c75bed91eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] The piece includes vignettes of Millenial hardship: …his dad, Steve, sold his company to a private equity firm about five years ago, which enabled him to retire comfortably in his mid-60s… Two years ago, Joe asked his dad for a loan. He wanted to start a lawn-care business and needed capital for commercial-grade equipment and a trailer, at least $15,000. He presented Steve with his business plan, which included a schedule to pay back the money. Still, Steve said “no.”… Joe hasn’t started the business. He works for a property-management firm and does landscaping work on the side to bring in extra money. But he has two young kids of his own and finds it difficult to save. “Without some initial help, it’s pretty unrealistic that I’ll ever be able to go the entrepreneurial route,” he says. recent_candidates.title: An Introduction to Richard Walther Darré
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://antelopehillpublishing.substack.com/p/an-introduction-to-richard-walther This is an article version of a thread we did on Twitter/X, which you can find here [ https://substack.com/redirect/782a5b35-982e-4398-8c83-f96cbc4b21a1?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ]. This information is presented for purposes of historical education. Richard Walther Darré was a famous theorist of the concept of “Blood and Soil” in National Socialist Germany, who would become a high-ranking member of Hitler’s government. We have published Darré’s two books in English for the first time: A New Nobility of Blood and Soil [ https://substack.com/redirect/4529906e-71a0-40c5-8335-ff6c0aa833f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] The Peasantry as the Lifeblood of the Nordic Race [ https://substack.com/redirect/7c9027b5-3eb4-4338-ae1c-35b3cd4e3000?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] Darré was born July 14, 1895 to a German family in Buenos Aires, and sent to school in Germany as a child. recent_candidates.title: Take an epic journey with our new Odyssey puzzle, toast the 21st amendment, and find more gifts for history buffs from The Reader’s Catalog
recent_candidates.excerpt: “Tell me the tale of a man, Muse, who had so many roundabout ways To wander, driven off course, after sacking Troy’s hallowed keep...” —The Odyssey by Homer, as translated by Daniel Mendelsohn The World of The Odyssey & Other Greek Myths: 1,000-Piece Puzzle This 1,000-piece puzzle shows the saga of Odysseus’s famed journey in one epic scene. Discover the heroes, gods, and monsters—from the the king of Ithaca and the Acheans to Athena, Poseidon, and Hermes, to Scylla and Charybdis, the Minotaur, and much more—while you piece together this beautiful image and help Odysseus return to Telemachus and Penelope. Shop Now [https://shop.nybooks.com/products/the-world-of-the-odyssey-and-other-greek-myths?_pos=2&_psq=odyssey&_ss=e&_v=1.0] The Real Women of Greek Myths: 1,000-Piece Puzzle In this beautifully detailed jigsaw puzzle, rediscover the lives and stories of the women of Greek myths, from Medusa and Medea to Helen, Eurydice, Aphrodite, Phaedra, Artemis, and others. As you build the puzzle, look for Pandora and her jar, Medusa with snakes for hair, and other characters with their signifiers and relevant details. recent_candidates.title: Will Global Warming Solve the Fertility Crisis?
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://www.stevesailer.net/p/will-global-warming-solve-the-fertility From the Australian Broadcasting Company: Climate change becoming a major driver of child marriage across Asia and the Pacific [ https://substack.com/redirect/3362c13f-a67e-46cb-9c27-85b7fd8b7674?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] What can’t climate change do? It’s a floor wax and a desert topping! By Claire Campbell Thursday 9 July In short: The growing intensity of natural disasters across Asia is leading to increasing numbers of child marriages of girls, according to aid organisations. They’re worried this year’s El Niño could exacerbate the issue of child marriage. What’s next? Advocates are calling for funding and resources around child marriage to be better integrated into climate change response and resilience programs. Runa was 15 when she married a man she had never met. Cyclone Remal had ripped through her camp in Bangladesh’s Cox’s Bazar region, killing her family’s chickens and ducks, which were the source of both their food and income. recent_candidates.title: Kazakhstan Bets on Iranian Port Investment
recent_candidates.excerpt: No Images? Click here https://fdd-new.cmail19.com/t/r-e-tutdjljl-oophjwr-it/ July 14, 2026 | Policy Brief Kazakhstan Bets on Iranian Port Investment [https://www.fdd.org/analysis/2026/07/14/kazakhstan-bets-on-iranian-port-investment/] Angela Howard [https://www.fdd.org/team/angela-howard/] | CEFP Research Analyst Antonia-Laura Pup | CEFP Intern Shahid Rajaee Port. (via Wikimedia) Landlocked Kazakhstan is seeking port access, and Astana is banking on Iran to provide it. On July 8, Kazakhstan signed an agreement [https://astanatimes.com/2026/07/kazakhstan-iran-sign-deal-to-build-logistics-terminal-at-shahid-rajaee-port/?utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email] to build a logistics and transport terminal at Iran’s largest seaport [https://www.gov.kz/memleket/entities/mfa-tehran/press/news/details/1249333] and sanctions evasion hub [https://www.unitedagainstnucleariran.com/irans-threat-to-maritime-security], Shahid Rajee Port in Bandar Abbas. Under the terms of the 27-year Build-Operate-Transfer agreement [https://www.investopedia.com/terms/b/botcontract.asp], Kazakhstan will construct the terminal in two years, operate it for 25, and then turn control over to Iran. recent_candidates.title: From convocation
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://www.pillarcatholic.com/p/from-convocation Hey everybody, Today’s the feast of St. Kateri Tekakwitha, and you’re reading The Tuesday Pillar Post. Well, sort of. I was out of the office yesterday, and I’m out still today, because I’m giving some talks at a convocation of priests from two dioceses in the Upper Midwest. I had the opportunity last night to spend time with many of those priests as they held a tournament, playing a complex card game which was brought to the United States by German immigrants in the last years of the 1800s, and is kept alive on the snow-swept prairies of our northern national borders. I did not enter the tournament, because I don’t know how to play, and a high-stakes presbyteral card night hardly seems the place to learn, especially since it’s a partner game, and I couldn’t ask anyone to sacrifice their run through the tournament to take a newbie as partner. Instead, I enjoyed drinks and conviviality with many of these men, and I was reminded how blessed we are in this country by the sort of priests who think nothing of routinely driving three hours to offer Mass for disparate communities — or, if they do think somethin

recent_candidates.title:
Mystery Grove Book Club Meeting 2: Always with Honor Chapters 4-6 Discussion
 Post
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://www.theconundrumcluster.com/p/mystery-grove-book-club-meeting-2 Hello everyone, I’m very happy to report that our first book club episode [ https://substack.com/redirect/98ae1fe2-375f-4787-ae23-6c7830600ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] was a huge success! One (paid) subscriber said “This is the greatest episode of MGs entire catalogue.” There is still a very lively dialogue ongoing in the comments section [ https://substack.com/redirect/98ae1fe2-375f-4787-ae23-6c7830600ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ], I really am grateful for all the hard work everyone is putting in on this. This week we’re moving on to the next three chapters of the book, which cover the White Army’s military high point during the Russian Civil War. This has been a lot of fun so far and I’m confident that will continue. It’s a lot more rewarding to read a book alongside a bunch of other people. If you haven’t started A;ways with Honor yet, there’s still time to catch up. You can order the brand-new edition (with new maps and appendices) here [ https://substack.com/redirect/035559b6-c6db-49cb-b846-4a5ee

recent_candidates.title: Dead Weight Center
recent_candidates.excerpt: Sponsored by Bloomsbury Academic [https://www.bloomsbury.com/us/] For the Review’s July 23 issue, Bryce Covert reads Dead Center [https://www.nybooks.com/articles/2026/07/23/compromised-values-dead-center-joe-manchin/], the unsettlingly titled memoir by former senator and former Democrat Joe Manchin. As Covert writes, the adamant centrist and underminer of his former party’s legislative efforts has written a similarly frustrating book: It feels as if he’s speaking from an alternate universe, one where the structures of our political system aren’t being torn down and our neighbors aren’t being terrorized before our eyes by authoritarian violence. His book may have nothing to say about the very real dangers we face, but it is starkly revealing of the worldview that got us here. And part of that worldview? An uncompromising insistence on compromising Democratic priorities: Manchin sees power as something to be used as a last resort, if at all.… This, to him, is somehow unthinkable—that a party elected on commitments to increase the minimum wage and send out stimulus checks would use the authority voters had handed it to actually do those things. Below, alongside Covert’s essay, we hav

recent_candidates.title: Before the Next Mythos Moment: The Case for an AI Threat Fusion Center
recent_candidates.excerpt: Earlier this spring, Claude’s developers found the cyber potential of Anthropic’s model, Mythos 5, hazardous enough to pump the brakes on model release, creating the Project Glasswing collaboration to give cyber defenders the upper hand through controlled access to the model. Despite these efforts, the U.S. government stepped in on June 12, 2026, and forced Anthropic to take down Mythos and its safeguarded form, Fable 5 — an action the company disputed, calling the trigger a narrow, nonuniversal jailbreak. A lack of standard processes, complex legal terrain, and information-sharing obstacles resulted in a “pickup game” where, absent a shared factual The post Before the Next Mythos Moment: The Case for an AI Threat Fusion Center appeared first on War on the Rocks .

recent_candidates.title: Breaking America’s Promise to Pacific Island Veterans
recent_candidates.excerpt: In January, Robson Henry died at the age of 66. Chances are, you have never heard of Henry. He was not famous, rich, or powerful. In many ways, Henry was no different from the thousands of young men and women who, at the age of 18, chose to serve by enlisting in the U.S. Army. Nor does the fact that he stayed in the Army for the next 26 years, serving multiple tours of duty in the Middle East, meaningfully distinguish him. And yet, Henry was special: Unlike the vast majority of his fellow soldiers, he was not from the The post Breaking America’s Promise to Pacific Island Veterans appeared first on War on the Rocks .

recent_candidates.title: NATO’s Unity on Paper and Fractures Beneath the Surface
recent_candidates.excerpt: On July 7-8, NATO held its 36th official summit in Ankara, Turkey. There were several consequential topics to discuss at this year’s meeting, chief among them the wars in Ukraine and Iran and the uncertain future of European defense and security. We asked five experts to assess the outcomes of the summit for the various parties involved.Liviu HorovitzAssociate Researcher at German Institute for Security and International AffairsThe key takeaway from Ankara is that even President Donald Trump is struggling to escape NATO’s strategic logic. He arrived attacking allies and questioning the alliance’s value, yet left reaffirming Article 5 and praising The post NATO’s Unity on Paper and Fractures Beneath the Surface appeared first on War on the Rocks .
